village_square.name: Village Square
village_square.description: The heart of the village, with a stone well and a few market stalls.
village_square.lore: This village survives by watching the forest edge, the ferry road, and whatever trouble moves between them.

shop.name: Shop
shop.description: A small wooden shop stocked with simple supplies and tools.
shop.lore: The village shop is less a market than a lifeline, keeping enough simple gear on hand that locals do not have to wait for the next safe caravan.

forest_path.name: Forest Path
forest_path.description: A narrow trail leading into the trees. Strange sounds echo nearby.
forest_path.lore: The first stretch of woodland road decides whether trade reaches the village at all, so even small threats matter here.

deep_forest.name: Deep Forest
deep_forest.description: The trees grow thick here, and the light is dim beneath the canopy.
deep_forest.lore: Old hunting paths and older fears overlap here, where wolves, raiders, and half-lost shrine roads all cross the same shadowed ground.

valewood.name: Valewood
valewood.description: Ward-marked trunks, hidden hearth lights, and careful footbridges reveal a living wood-settlement built to watch the deep paths without stripping them bare.
valewood.lore: Valewood keeps a quieter line of Valerion, where clan memory, animal paths, and living wards are all treated as duties owed to the realm rather than secrets hoarded for pride.
valewood.economy_note: Trade here runs on herbs, furs, warded timber, and the kind of trust that takes longer to earn than coin.

major_hub.name: Valewood
major_hub.progression_role: Valewood roots the forest side of the campaign in a defended culture instead of a chain of hostile clearings.
major_hub.travel_identity: Light forest coaches and oath-guided trail caravans keep the wood roads usable without breaking them.
major_hub.campaign_role: Valewood gives the player a true forest hub once road trouble gives way to deeper clan and ward concerns.

old_watchtower.name: Old Watchtower
old_watchtower.description: A broken stone tower rises above the trees, quiet but still standing.
old_watchtower.lore: The watchtower once gave the village warning over the road and treeline, so even half-ruined it still feels like part of the same border watch.

ruined_shrine.name: Abandoned Shrine
ruined_shrine.description: Cracked pillars and faded carvings mark an abandoned shrine deep in the woods.
ruined_shrine.lore: The shrine belonged to an older order of keepers, and locals still speak as if the stones remember more than they should.

river_crossing.name: River Crossing
river_crossing.description: A cold river cuts across the road here, bridged by old pilings and a rope-guided ferry.
river_crossing.lore: Whoever controls the crossing controls what reaches the village, the ruins, and the newer market roads beyond.

old_ruins.name: Old Ruins
old_ruins.description: Broken arches and fallen walls ring a courtyard where faded carvings still cling to the stone.
old_ruins.lore: These ruins predate the current roads, and scholars argue whether they once guarded the shrine or warned people away from it.

bandit_camp.name: Bandit Camp
bandit_camp.description: Low tents and ash-black fire pits mark a camp built for raiding rather than staying.
bandit_camp.lore: The camp survives because the roads are useful and because fear travels faster than any wagon train.

whispering_cave.name: Whispering Cave
whispering_cave.description: Cold air slips through narrow stone throats, making every sound in the cave feel half-spoken.
whispering_cave.lore: People say the cave carries voices farther than it should, which is enough reason for most locals to stay out.

town_gate.name: Town Gate
town_gate.description: Stonewatch's gate rises in pale blockwork above the road, built to welcome trade while making trouble slow itself down.
town_gate.lore: Stonewatch holds because caravans, patrols, pilgrims, and hunters all need the same wall, the same market, and the same rules at the end of a long road.
town_gate.economy_note: Caravan tolls, gate checks, and road safety all start here.

market_square.name: Market Square
market_square.description: Stonewatch's market square is tight with awnings, handcarts, and callers who know the road well enough to price it by the mile.
market_square.lore: The square is where forest goods become town coin, where travelers overhear their next lead, and where Stonewatch proves it is more than a checkpoint with walls.
market_square.economy_note: The square sets the town's mood: food, salvage, and rumor all change hands here first.

major_hub.name: Stonewatch
major_hub.progression_role: Stonewatch is the frontier order made visible: a market-wall hub where contracts, supply, and civic duty keep the realm from coming apart.
major_hub.travel_identity: Carriage ledgers, caravan guards, and posted route clerks move people along the safer held roads.
major_hub.campaign_role: Stonewatch anchors the early campaign and turns local survival into wider frontier service.

blacksmith.name: Blacksmith
blacksmith.description: Heat, hammer-song, and iron filings fill the forge, where practical work is stacked in racks before it ever reaches a display wall.
blacksmith.lore: Stonewatch's smiths live on road demand: nails for wagons, rivets for armor, and hard promises about which steel will still hold after a bad week.
blacksmith.economy_note: The forge turns salvaged iron and hard coin into gear people trust on the road.

mage_shop.name: Mage Shop
mage_shop.description: Quiet shelves of glass, vellum, and warded tools keep the mage shop feeling more measured than grand, with every item set where careless hands cannot reach it first.
mage_shop.lore: Stonewatch's arcane trade stays modest on purpose: enough to arm novices, mend field kits, and keep the shrine orders supplied without pretending the frontier is a college.
mage_shop.economy_note: Arcane work here is practical frontier trade, not ornament: tonics, foci, and careful reagents.

weapon_shop.name: Weapon Shop
weapon_shop.description: Racks of bows, spears, and field blades line a narrow room built for hunters, scouts, and travelers who expect the road to argue back.
weapon_shop.lore: The weapon shop survives by outfitting people who need clean range, quick reach, or something cheap enough to risk on uncertain roads.
weapon_shop.economy_note: This shop trades in reach, timing, and gear light enough to carry through bad country.

inn.name: Inn
inn.description: Warm light, stew steam, and tired voices make the inn feel like the first room in miles where nobody needs to keep moving.
inn.lore: Road towns are measured by their inns as much as their walls, and Stonewatch keeps one good enough that caravans plan around it.
inn.economy_note: Beds, hot meals, and safe nights keep caravans spending coin in town instead of pushing on hungry.

town_shrine.name: Shrine
town_shrine.description: A compact stone shrine stands off the market lane, quiet enough to gather breath without pretending the road beyond its walls has gone anywhere.
town_shrine.lore: Stonewatch's shrine is less grand than old woodland sanctums, but it remains where guards, traders, and pilgrims all come to steady themselves before the next road.
town_shrine.economy_note: Pilgrims and guards leave offerings here, and the shrine quietly feeds the town's arcane supply lines.

emberfall.name: Emberfall
emberfall.description: Ash-lanterns burn above stepped cloisters and guarded courtyards where shrine folk, watch escorts, and ashward carriers gather before taking the harder road onward.
emberfall.lore: Emberfall survives as a sworn fire-break in the realm, a place where ward-keepers and road servants refuse to let the ash roads become a lawless wound in Valerion.
emberfall.economy_note: Offerings, sealed reagents, and escorted ash-road freight pass through Emberfall under strict records and stricter ritual care.

major_hub.name: Emberfall
major_hub.progression_role: Emberfall links shrine progression, ash-lore, and the vault approach into one ordered frontier hub.
major_hub.travel_identity: Shrine escorts and warded caravans move only under rite, ledger, and watchful escort.
major_hub.campaign_role: Emberfall gives the Cult of Ash storyline a defended counterpart before the late vault push.

watch_barracks.name: Watch Barracks
watch_barracks.description: Open racks, chalk boards, and bundled kit line the barracks hall where Stonewatch turns road worry into patrols and supply counts.
watch_barracks.lore: The barracks keep Stonewatch honest: not a grand fortress, but a working watch-post where discipline means the roads still function tomorrow.
watch_barracks.economy_note: Patrol supplies, levy accounts, and frontier contracts pass through the barracks before they reach the roads.

ironridge_pass.name: Ironridge Pass
ironridge_pass.description: Switchback stone roads climb toward dwarf-cut walls while cold wind carries forge smoke down the pass.
ironridge_pass.lore: Ironridge's pass is where caravan hope meets hard stone, watched closely because every loaded wagon draws either trade or trouble.

ironridge_square.name: Ironridge Square
ironridge_square.description: Heavy lintels, slate roofs, and carved clan marks give the hold's square a practical grandeur built to last centuries, not seasons.
ironridge_square.lore: Ironridge lives by ore, contract, and memory, measuring worth by what survives the road and what holds true after the hammer falls.
ironridge_square.economy_note: Ore contracts, wagon repairs, and oath-bound freight keep the hold tied to every lower road that still matters.

major_hub.name: Ironridge Hold
major_hub.progression_role: Ironridge is the mountain stronghold where Valerion's line becomes stone, labor, and armed convoy discipline.
major_hub.travel_identity: Escorted caravans and ridge wagons run on contract law, not speed.
major_hub.campaign_role: The hold opens mid-game martial progression, contract pressure, and the Gorgos line.

ironridge_forge.name: Ironridge Forge
ironridge_forge.description: Banked coals, rune-cut anvils, and orderly steel racks make the hold's forge feel like a workshop built inside a heartbeat.
ironridge_forge.lore: The hold's forge is where caravan contracts become iron certainty, and where visiting traders learn quickly that dwarven steel is priced by labor, not flattery.

stormbreak_cliffs.name: Stormbreak Cliffs
stormbreak_cliffs.description: Salt wind lashes the cliff road while shattered watch posts stare down at black water and broken surf.
stormbreak_cliffs.lore: Stormbreak's coast is useful, dangerous, and never fully quiet, with smugglers, wreck-scavengers, and honest scouts all reading the same tide for different reasons.

stormbreak.name: Stormbreak
stormbreak.description: Harbor bells, ferry chains, and wind-cut awnings mark a coastal hold where every arrival is weighed against weather, law, and what the sea might still be hiding.
stormbreak.lore: Stormbreak stands because its harbor folk keep choosing to hold the western sea-road for Valerion, mending ferries, posting watchfires, and refusing to yield the last safe landing.
stormbreak.economy_note: Salt freight, rescue fees, and tide-timed caravans keep Stormbreak alive as both shield and market.

major_hub.name: Stormbreak
major_hub.progression_role: Stormbreak opens the coastward line of the campaign and roots Tideborn identity in a living harbor culture.
major_hub.travel_identity: Ferries, seawall coaches, and tide-certified cargo caravans carry people when the coast allows it.
major_hub.campaign_role: Stormbreak ties the frontier inland roads to the sea and stages the approach toward the ash coast.

stormbreak_cove.name: Stormbreak Cove
stormbreak_cove.description: A narrow cove of wreck timber, tide pools, and wave-cut stone gives raiders and scavengers too many places to hide.
stormbreak_cove.lore: The cove keeps what storms and raids leave behind, which is why every patrol through it feels like checking the coast's teeth for fresh blood.

ashen_stair.name: Ashen Stair
ashen_stair.description: Blackened steps descend beneath a cliffside arch whose seals were broken from within long ago.
ashen_stair.lore: The stair marks the edge of a buried sanctuary that burned once, was sealed, and has lately begun drawing cult marks and careful footsteps back to it.

vaultreach.name: Vaultreach
vaultreach.description: Layered ward-stones, supply sheds, and quiet watchfires surround a guarded staging court built for people who mean to enter the ash roads and still come back with order intact.
vaultreach.lore: Vaultreach exists because Valerion's most dangerous places still need a line held before them, with scribes, wardens, and carriers standing the duty nobody mistakes for glory.
vaultreach.economy_note: Everything here is practical: rations, seals, salvage ledgers, and whatever the vault has not yet taken back.

major_hub.name: Vaultreach
major_hub.progression_role: Vaultreach is the late-game mustering yard where shrine duty, relic recovery, and vault contracts finally meet.
major_hub.travel_identity: Restricted ward-convoy transit moves only under seal, escort, and written authority.
major_hub.campaign_role: Vaultreach gives the Ashen Vault a living frontier edge instead of a disconnected dungeon endpoint.

ashen_vault.name: Ashen Vault
ashen_vault.description: Soot-dark halls and half-melted shrine metal stretch into a vault where old devotion and newer corruption have fused together.
ashen_vault.lore: Whatever the vault guarded, the Cult of Ash now treats it as a reliquary and proving ground, leaving old stone guardians to share the dark with living zealots.

ashwood_ruins.name: Ashwood Ruins
ashwood_ruins.description: Collapsed ward-stones and old halls sink beneath Ashwood roots.
ashwood_ruins.lore: The ruins once marked a guarded shrine route. Now hunters use them as a proving ground before deeper contracts.

ironridge_deep_mine.name: Ironridge Deep Mine
ironridge_deep_mine.description: A sealed mine entrance reinforced with fresh hold sigils.
ironridge_deep_mine.lore: Ironridge closed the lower shaft after repeated raider pressure and unstable veins. Recovery crews still post bounties for secure access.

emberfall_catacombs.name: Emberfall Shrine Catacombs
emberfall_catacombs.description: Old ward tunnels descend beneath Emberfall's shrine district.
emberfall_catacombs.lore: Shrine keepers interred failed ward relics below Emberfall. Only trained teams are meant to enter when ash pressure spikes.

stormbreak_smuggler_caves.name: Stormbreak Smuggler Caves
stormbreak_smuggler_caves.description: A hidden shoreline route used by raiders and black-market crews.
stormbreak_smuggler_caves.lore: Stormbreak wardens watch these caves whenever harbor manifests go missing. Smuggler crews rotate lookouts by tide.

vaultreach_lower_archives.name: Vaultreach Lower Archives
vaultreach_lower_archives.description: A sealed archive descent beneath Vaultreach's relic quarter.
vaultreach_lower_archives.lore: The lower archives catalog pre-fall relic movements. Access is tightly controlled and often tied to high-risk contracts.
